questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is Kumbhalgarh Fort located?
questions.explanation: Kumbhalgarh Fort stands in the Aravalli hills of Rajasthan, in the Rajsamand district.

questions.q: Kumbhalgarh is most famous for its enormous perimeter wall, which has earned it what popular nickname?
questions.explanation: Its roughly 36-km-long perimeter wall is often called the Great Wall of India.

questions.q: Approximately how long is the perimeter wall of Kumbhalgarh Fort?
questions.explanation: The fort's outer wall runs roughly 36 kilometres, among the longest continuous walls in the world.

questions.q: Kumbhalgarh Fort was a major stronghold of which Rajput kingdom?
questions.explanation: Kumbhalgarh was one of the principal forts of the kingdom of Mewar.

questions.q: Which famous Mewar warrior-king was born at Kumbhalgarh Fort?
questions.explanation: Maharana Pratap, the legendary ruler of Mewar, was born at Kumbhalgarh in 1540.

questions.q: In which century was Kumbhalgarh Fort principally built?
questions.explanation: The fort was built during the 15th century under Mewar ruler Rana Kumbha.

questions.q: Kumbhalgarh Fort is built on the ranges of which mountain system?
questions.explanation: The fort sits on a hilltop in the Aravalli Range of western India.

questions.q: What type of structure is Kumbhalgarh?
questions.explanation: Kumbhalgarh is a hill fort, perched high in the Aravalli hills of Rajasthan.

questions.q: Kumbhalgarh is part of a UNESCO World Heritage listing covering forts of which group?
questions.explanation: Kumbhalgarh is one of the six forts inscribed as the 'Hill Forts of Rajasthan' by UNESCO.

questions.q: The fort is named 'Kumbhalgarh' after which ruler?
questions.explanation: The fort takes its name from Rana Kumbha, the Mewar ruler who built it.

questions.q: Which Mewar ruler commissioned the construction of Kumbhalgarh Fort?
questions.explanation: Rana Kumbha (Maharana Kumbhakarna) of Mewar built the fort in the 15th century.

questions.q: Kumbhalgarh's perimeter wall is said to be so broad in places that several of which could ride side by side along the top?
questions.explanation: The ramparts are famously wide enough that several horses (often said to be about eight) could ride abreast along the top.

questions.q: Which architect is traditionally credited with designing Kumbhalgarh Fort for Rana Kumbha?
questions.explanation: The architect Mandan is traditionally credited with designing Kumbhalgarh, and he wrote treatises on architecture.

questions.q: The highest building within the fort, a palace from which the ruler's surroundings could be viewed, is known by what name?
questions.explanation: The Badal Mahal (Palace of Clouds) crowns the fort at its highest point.

questions.q: Roughly how many temples are said to lie within the walls of Kumbhalgarh Fort?
questions.explanation: The fort is said to enclose more than 300 ancient temples, both Hindu and Jain.

questions.q: When the infant Udai Singh II was smuggled to safety, Kumbhalgarh served as his refuge, protected by which loyal nursemaid of legend?
questions.explanation: Panna Dai is remembered for protecting the young prince Udai Singh, who was kept safe at Kumbhalgarh.

questions.q: Kumbhalgarh's hilltop location gives it a commanding altitude of roughly how high above sea level?
questions.explanation: The fort stands at roughly 1,100 metres above sea level on a ridge of the Aravallis.

questions.q: Within Mewar's defensive history, Kumbhalgarh is regarded as second in importance only to which other great fort?
questions.explanation: Kumbhalgarh was considered the second most important fort of Mewar after Chittorgarh.

questions.q: The main entrance gate to the fort complex is commonly known by which name?
questions.explanation: Ram Pol (Ram Gate) is the principal entrance into the fort.

questions.q: The fort's massive walls and bastions were primarily constructed using which material?
questions.explanation: The walls were built largely of stone and rubble masonry quarried from the surrounding hills.

questions.q: Today the area surrounding Kumbhalgarh Fort is protected as what kind of conservation area?
questions.explanation: The fort is surrounded by the Kumbhalgarh Wildlife Sanctuary, home to wolves, leopards and other fauna.

questions.q: In which year were the Hill Forts of Rajasthan, including Kumbhalgarh, inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List?
questions.explanation: The Hill Forts of Rajasthan were inscribed by UNESCO in 2013.

questions.q: Rana Kumbha is said to have largely built Kumbhalgarh over which approximate period?
questions.explanation: Rana Kumbha is generally credited with building the fort over roughly the 1440s and 1450s.

questions.q: Rana Kumbha is credited with constructing or repairing a large share of Mewar's forts. Roughly how many of the region's forts does tradition attribute to him?
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Rana Kumbha built or restored around 32 forts in Mewar, of which Kumbhalgarh is the grandest.

questions.q: In 1577-78, in the years following the Battle of Haldighati, Kumbhalgarh was besieged and captured by the forces of which Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Akbar's Mughal forces, led by Shahbaz Khan, besieged and captured Kumbhalgarh around 1577-78, after the Battle of Haldighati (1576).

questions.q: A famous legend says the fort wall's first attempts kept collapsing until what act, advised by a holy man, allowed building to succeed?
options: A diamond was buried in the foundation
A voluntary human sacrifice was made
The wall was rebuilt only at night
A river was diverted around the hill
questions.explanation: Legend holds that construction succeeded only after a voluntary human sacrifice, with a shrine marking the spot where the head fell.

questions.q: Rana Kumbha was a noted patron of the arts; which musical-architectural treatise tradition associates with his court or name?
questions.explanation: Rana Kumbha was a scholar-king associated with works such as the Sangita Raja, reflecting his patronage of music and learning.

questions.q: A defining feature of Kumbhalgarh's defences is that the fort is enclosed by how many fortified gateways along its approaches?
questions.explanation: The fort is defended by a series of seven great fortified gateways guarding its approaches.

questions.q: Within the fort stands an early shrine notable for being one of the oldest Hindu temples there, dedicated to which deity associated with Mewar?
questions.explanation: The Neelkanth Mahadev temple, dedicated to Shiva, is among the most important and ancient shrines inside the fort.

questions.q: The frontier marked by Kumbhalgarh historically separated the territories of Mewar from which neighbouring Rajput kingdom?
questions.explanation: Kumbhalgarh guarded the frontier between Mewar and the neighbouring kingdom of Marwar (Jodhpur).
